jsonLdWeb.name: Beehive Yield Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Estimate honey production per hive based on region, hive type, and beekeeper experience with revenue and wax yield calculations.

mainEntity.name: How much honey does one hive produce per year?

acceptedAnswer.text: A typical Langstroth hive produces 30-60 lbs of surplus honey per year in temperate climates. Experienced beekeepers in optimal locations can harvest 60-100+ lbs. First-year hives often produce little to no surplus.

mainEntity.name: How many jars of honey per hive?

acceptedAnswer.text: At 50 lbs of honey, you get about 40 twelve-ounce jars or 50 eight-ounce jars. Honey weighs about 12 oz per cup (1.5 lbs per pint). One pound fills roughly one 16-oz jar.

mainEntity.name: How much is honey worth per pound?

acceptedAnswer.text: Local raw honey sells for $8-$15 per pound at farmers markets. Specialty varieties (wildflower, buckwheat, sourwood) can fetch $15-$25 per pound. Wholesale to stores is typically $5-$8 per pound.

mainEntity.name: How much beeswax does a hive produce?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bees produce about 1 lb of wax for every 6-7 lbs of honey. A hive yielding 50 lbs of honey generates roughly 2-3 lbs of harvestable wax from cappings. Beeswax sells for $8-$15 per pound.

mainEntity.name: What type of hive produces the most honey?

acceptedAnswer.text: Langstroth hives are the most productive, averaging 30-60 lbs per year. Top bar hives produce 15-35 lbs, and Warre hives 20-40 lbs. Langstroth hives are easier to manage for maximum harvest.

First-year hives typically produce little surplus honey, as bees need to build comb and establish the colony. Most beekeepers recommend not harvesting the first year to ensure bees have enough stores for winter.

howItWorks:
How the Beehive Yield Calculator Works

This calculator estimates honey production based on hive type, regional climate, and beekeeper experience.

Yield = Base Rate × Region Factor × Experience Factor × Hives
  • Langstroth: Base 50 lbs/hive, most productive design
  • Top Bar: Base 25 lbs/hive, more wax per honey lb
  • Warré: Base 30 lbs/hive, natural comb building
  • Wax yield: ~1 lb wax per 6-7 lbs honey harvested
  • Revenue: Calculated at $10/lb (local farmers market average)
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How much honey does one hive produce?

A Langstroth hive typically produces 30-60 lbs of surplus honey per year in temperate climates. Experienced beekeepers can harvest 60-100+ lbs. First-year hives usually produce little surplus.

How many jars of honey per hive?

At 50 lbs of honey per hive, you get about 40 twelve-ounce jars or 50 eight-ounce jars. One pound of honey fills roughly one 16-oz jar. Honey is denser than water at about 12 oz per cup.

How much is honey worth per pound?

Local raw honey sells for $8-$15 per pound at farmers markets and $15-$25 for specialty varieties. Wholesale to stores runs $5-$8 per pound. Value-added products like creamed honey fetch higher prices.

How much beeswax does a hive produce?

Roughly 1 lb of wax per 6-7 lbs of honey from capping wax. A hive producing 50 lbs of honey yields 2-3 lbs of harvestable wax. Top bar hives produce more wax relative to honey than Langstroth hives.

What hive type produces the most honey?

Langstroth hives are the most productive at 30-60 lbs per year. Top bar hives average 15-35 lbs and Warre hives 20-40 lbs. Langstroth's standardized frames make management and harvesting most efficient.

Do first-year hives produce honey?

First-year hives rarely produce surplus honey. The colony spends its energy building comb, raising brood, and storing enough for winter. Most beekeepers wait until the second year to harvest.

sections.title: What Is the Beehive Yield Calculator?
sections.body: The Beehive Yield Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your number of hives, region / climate, hive type, you get instant results including honey yield, jars, jars. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting honey yield right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?

It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

faq.question: Is the Beehive Yield Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What number of hives should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate number of hives value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Beehive Yield Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

faq.question: What does honey yield mean in my results?
faq.answer: The honey yield value represents the primary output of the calculation based on your inputs. Use it as a baseline figure for your practical calculation planning and decision-making.
